title: Blind Masseuse
condition: I heard there was a new blind massage parlor. Dinglingling, I died.
solution: I went to the new blind massage parlor for a massage. During the session, my phone rang. The masseuse naturally handed it to me without asking where to place it, which made me suspect that he might not be blind. I thought about pretending not to know, but the caller ID made him realize that I had been a police officer. I lost my eyesight while fighting their criminal activities, and I came to this massage parlor to learn about the lives of blind people. However, I did not expect that by entering this parlor, I had walked into a trap.

zh.title: 盲人按摩师
zh.condition: 听说新开了一家盲人按摩店。叮铃铃铃,我死了。
zh.solution: 我来到新开的盲人按摩店按摩,在按摩过程中,我的手机铃声响了,他自然地递给我,但是竟然没有问我手机放哪了,我意识到他可能是假盲人,原想假装不知道,却没想到手机来电显示的内容使他知道了我以前是警察,我的眼睛由于在打击他们团体的犯罪活动时受伤,所以我失明了,我来到盲人按摩店原想了解一些盲人生活技巧,但是没想到此时的我进入到他的店却成了自投罗网。

key_facts: The fake blind masseuse harbors deadly intent
The phone call reveals the police officer's identity
A blind officer falls into danger
Danger hidden behind learning about blind people's lives
A trap set for the unsuspecting

title: Frantic Knocking
condition: The person outside frantically knocked on the door; he ignored it. He heard a hammer smashing the door, and soon after, screams came from outside the door.
solution: He was a pilot suffering from depression. The doctor diagnosed his depression and reported it to the airline, hoping he would be grounded, but the company didn't care. One day on a civilian flight, he told the co-pilot to go to the restroom, sending him away. It was already the cruising phase, which should have been very safe. When the co-pilot returned, he found the cockpit door locked. He frantically knocked, but the pilot inside was unmoved. He started smashing the door, but airplane doors are very strong. Passengers gradually understood what was happening, and at the moment of impact with the mountain, the black box recorded the screams of all passengers.

zh.title: 发疯敲门
zh.condition:
外面的人发疯敲门,他置之不理。
他听到了锤子砸门的声音,不久后,门外传来惨叫。
zh.solution: 他是一个有抑郁症的机长。医生检查出了他的抑郁症,并报告给了航空公司,希望他可以停飞,但公司并不在意。某天民航,他对副机长说,你去上个厕所吧,就支走了副机长。当时已经是巡航阶段,应该是很安全的。副机长回来时,发现驾驶室门锁了。他发疯敲门,门里的机长无动于衷。他开始砸门,但飞机门是很坚固的。乘客们逐渐明白发生了什么,在即将撞山的瞬间,小黑盒录下了所有乘客的尖叫。

key_facts: The story takes place inside an airplane cockpit
The person inside the door is the pilot who intentionally locked it
The person outside the door is the co-pilot trying to enter
The pilot suffered from depression and was ignored
The final screams came from the plane crash

title: The Cash Heist
condition: I rushed into the third-floor room. The window was rattling in the wind. There was a large hole in the floor with an umbrella next to it. When I went down to the second floor to check, my heart sank.
solution: I am a security guard for a vault. On patrol, I noticed the third-floor window was open. Upon entering the third floor, I saw signs of a break-in. When I went to the second floor, I found the gold bars were gone. The thief entered through the third-floor window and first drilled a small hole in the floor. To prevent the debris from making noise and alerting security, they inserted an umbrella through the small hole and opened it directly underneath. The debris from drilling the larger hole then fell into the umbrella. Once the hole was large enough, the thief could enter the second-floor vault, steal the gold, and escape back through the hole to the third floor.

zh.title: 现金盗窃案
zh.condition: 我慌忙跑进三楼房间,窗户被吹得劈啪作响,地上有个大洞,洞旁放着一把伞,当我来到二楼查看情况后,我的心情非常糟糕。
zh.solution: 我是金库安保人员,巡视时发现三楼窗户处于打开状态。来到三楼后发现有窃贼作案痕迹,来到二楼发现金条已经丢失。盗贼从三楼窗口进入,先在地板上向下凿出一个小洞。为了避免凿洞时产生的碎屑造成更大的动静引来保安,用一把伞伸入凿出的小洞在其正下方打开,从三楼凿洞掉落的碎屑会落入伞中。凿出大洞后,窃贼便能进入二楼金库,盗走金条,再原路从大洞返回三楼逃离。

key_facts: My' identity is a security guard.
The stolen items were on the second floor.
The thief entered from the third floor.
The umbrella had a very specific purpose.
The position of the hole is crucial.
The umbrella was used to catch debris.

title: Falling Object
condition: The joyful girl looked up and cried; the sorrowful old man looked up and smiled.
solution: The falling object was a child's drawing, covered in colorful crayon marks, depicting a family holding hands. A gust of wind had blown the paper from an open window upstairs, and it floated down gently. The old man was sitting on a park bench, enjoying the quiet afternoon sun. He was a bit lonely, as his children were not around. The brightly colored drawing landed right at his feet. He bent down to pick it up, saw the naive yet joyful strokes, and couldn't help but smile a warm, kind smile. A few minutes earlier, the little girl had been drawing by the window upstairs, cherishing her newly completed 'family portrait.' When a gust of wind snatched the paper away, she immediately burst into tears.

zh.title: 掉东西
zh.condition: 满心欢喜的女孩抬头看,哭了;一脸愁容的爷爷抬头看,笑了。
zh.solution: 掉下来的东西是一张画满了彩色蜡笔痕迹的儿童画纸,画上是一个一家人手拉手的场景。这张画被一阵风从楼上某个开着的窗户吹了出来,缓缓飘落。老爷爷,正坐在花园的长椅上,安静地享受着午后的阳光。他有些孤单,儿女不在身边。这时,这张色彩鲜艳的画纸恰好飘落在他脚边。他弯腰捡起,看到了画上那稚嫩却充满快乐的笔触,忍不住露出了慈祥、温暖的笑容。几分钟前,小女孩在楼上的窗边画画,非常珍爱这幅刚刚完成的“全家福”。一阵风吹来,画纸不小心被卷走飞出了窗外。小女孩立刻伤心地大哭起来。

The object is a piece of paper.
There is a drawing on the paper.

title: In Vain
condition: On Mother's Day, I wanted to make a unique gift for my mother. I made a flower wreath and prepared her favorite fruits. But when the gift was ready, my mother cried in sorrow.
solution: On Mother's Day, I hadn't seen my mother for a long time, so I decided to walk to town to surprise her. However, I didn't have enough money for a taxi, but I remembered the way. I just needed to follow the main road. Along the way, I was filled with joy, admiring the scenery. Seeing beautiful flowers, I thought they must be what my mother liked, so I picked them to weave into a flower wreath. When I saw the fruits my mother had mentioned before, I picked them, thinking she would be delighted to taste them. But due to my carelessness, I fell while picking the fruit and hit my head on a stone. In the wilderness, no one discovered my bleeding. I fell into a hallucination, seeing my mother welcoming me with a surprised and happy face, wearing the flower wreath I made and eating the fruits I picked, filling my heart with happiness. That night, my grandfather informed my mother of my disappearance. With the help of the police, my mother saw my body.

zh.title: 一场空
zh.condition: 母亲节,我想为母亲制作一份独特的礼物。我做好了花环,亲手备好她爱吃的水果。但是礼物做好了,母亲却伤心哭了。
zh.solution: 母亲节。我已经很久没有见到母亲了,于是决定徒步走到镇里,给她一个惊喜。然而,我身上没有足够的钱打车,但我记得这条路,只需要沿着大路走就好。在路上,我怀着喜悦的心情,欣赏着沿途的风景。看到美丽的花朵,我想这一定是母亲喜欢的,于是采下来编成花环;见到母亲曾经提过的果实,我上前摘下,心想着母亲尝到一定会很高兴。但是,因为我的粗心大意,在采摘果实时不慎跌倒,头部磕到地上的石头。在这片荒山野岭中,没人发现我不断流淌的鲜血。我陷入了幻觉,看到母亲满脸惊喜地迎接我,戴着我亲手编的花环,吃着我摘的果实,心里充满了幸福。当晚,外公向母亲告知我的失踪的情况,在警察的帮助下母亲见到了我的尸体。

key_facts: Made a flower wreath and prepared fruits for mother
Walked to town to surprise mother
Fell and hit head while picking fruit
Bled to death in the wilderness without being noticed

title: Her
condition: New bruises appeared. 'I love you but we can't be together,' she said, leading me to secluded cliffs. 'No one would know if someone fell here.' When bruises reappeared, I was terrified.
solution: A beautiful manipulator, she faked abuse (creating bruises) to lure me into killing her husband. After enjoying our time together, new bruises signaled I would be her next victim to eliminate.

zh.title: 她
zh.condition: 她身上又出现淤青了。她说喜欢我,可惜不能和我在一起。她把我带到那片海,说她想自杀。这里可真偏僻啊,就算掉下去也不会有人知道吧。她身上又出现淤青了。我好害怕。
zh.solution: 美貌是我的利器。从我懂事起,就有无数男人为我神魂颠倒。我享受着他们爱慕的眼神,更沉迷于那种众星捧月的感觉。在这之中,有一个男人格外的帅气幽默。于是我选择了和他结婚。刚开始,我确实很快乐。可是,快乐是短暂的。没多久,我就腻了这种生活,看中了下一个猎物。我诱捕了这个男人(“我”)。毕竟对我来说,这很简单,只要勾勾手指就能做到。可是,我和他之间还有一个阻碍,那就是我的丈夫。我开始伪装成被家暴的样子(制造淤青),并且把男人(“我”)带到了偏僻的海边,暗示他可以神不知鬼不觉的杀了我的丈夫。果然,我实现了愿望。在这之后,我和男人(“我”)度过了一段快乐的时光。可是,我又开始厌倦了,我知道,我该开始寻找下一个猎物了。(所以她身上再次出现淤青,是为下一个猎物做准备,暗示“我”家暴她,而“我”害怕是因为意识到自己可能就是下一个被除掉的“丈夫”。)

title: Taking out the Trash
condition: Mom is going to the hospital tomorrow, and I'm very scared. So I rush to take out the trash every day.
solution: I once asked Mom where I came from. Mom told me I was picked up from a trash can, and I believed it. That day, Mom told me I would soon have a younger sibling. I was very worried that Mom would ignore me once the new sibling arrived. To prevent Mom from picking up a new sibling from the trash can, I rushed to take out the trash every day.

title: Destiny
condition: Every night, the deity would come into my dreams, asking me to wear that crown. Many wanted to wear that crown, but I didn't. Giant waves rose on the calm sea, and amidst lightning and thunder, I put on the blood-soaked crown. The cowardly me shed tears.
solution: Our kingdom was protected by a deity, and each king was chosen by the deity. Children across the country became king candidates and participated in training. Everyone hoped to wear that crown, symbolizing power, and become the deity's chosen one, except for me. However, if this crown was worn by someone not chosen by the deity, the wearer would die. The deity appeared in my dreams, enticing and coercing me to wear that crown. I knew I was chosen, but I didn't want to be king, to endure boundless loneliness and responsibility. My evasion angered the deity, causing strong winds and thunderstorms in our kingdom. Others frantically put on the crown hoping to appease the deity's wrath, only to die one by one. The cowardly me finally recognized reality and accepted my destiny to wear the crown when my dear friend died in front of me.

zh.title: 命运
zh.condition: 每晚神明都会来到我的梦中,让我带上那顶皇冠。想要带上那顶皇冠的人很多,但是我不想。平静的海面掀起巨浪,电闪雷鸣中我带上了满是鲜血的皇冠,懦弱的我留下了泪。
zh.solution: 我们的国度是被神明庇护的,每一任国王都是由神明选出。全国的小孩都成为国王的备选人,参加培训。大家都希望能带上那顶象征权力的皇冠,成为神的幸运儿,除了我。可是这顶皇冠如果不是被神选中的王带上,佩戴者便会死亡。神明在我的梦中出现,引诱我、胁迫我带上那顶皇冠,我知道我被选中了,但我不想成为为王,忍受无际的孤独寂寞和责任。我的逃避引来了神的不满,于是我们的国度狂风大作、雷雨交加。其他人疯了似的戴上皇冠希望平息神的怒火,却一一死去,懦弱的我终于在我的好友死在面前时认清了现实、接受了戴上皇冠的命运。

title: Black Cat's Gratitude

solution: I often saw that black cat downstairs, but due to business trips, I could only feed it. That day, returning from a business trip, I found many dead mice on my balcony. I instinctively thought of my frequently feuding neighbor, then rushed into his house. Amidst screams and police sirens, I saw the black cat. title: Bowling
condition: Mom took me bowling, and I was very happy, but I only played once, and mom never took me again.
solution: I am a blind child. Since I was little, my dad often beat and scolded my mom. Dad had promised to take me bowling, but that promise remained unfulfilled for a long time. Finally, one day, Mom couldn't stand Dad anymore. During an argument, she killed Dad and dismembered him. Mom took me to a very secluded place, held Dad's head, and asked me: "Baby, do you want to play bowling with Mommy?" I was very happy, so I threw Dad's head, which was handed to me, like a bowling ball. While I was "playing," Mom secretly threw Dad's body parts in various places in the mountains. The next day, Mom was found and arrested by the police. She never took me bowling again.

zh.title: 保龄球
zh.condition: 妈妈带我去玩保龄球,我很开心,但我只玩了一次,之后妈妈就再没带我去玩过。
zh.solution: 我是一个盲眼小孩,从我小时候起,爸爸就经常打骂妈妈。本来爸爸答应了要带我去玩保龄球的,但这个承诺一直拖欠了很久也没有实现。终于有一天,妈妈受不了爸爸,在争吵之中把爸爸杀了并分尸。妈妈带着我跑到很偏僻的地方,拿着爸爸的头问我:"宝宝,要跟妈妈一起玩保龄球吗?"我很开心,于是把递过来的爸爸的头当成了保龄球扔了出去,我"打球"的时候,妈妈偷偷把爸爸的尸块扔到了山里的各个地方。第二天,妈妈被警察找上来抓了,她再也没有带我去玩保龄球了。

title: Cost
condition: There's a contract that can achieve miracles and fulfill any 'hope,' at the cost of becoming a magical warrior, destined to fight despairing witches for life, protecting the world, but this war is endless.
solution: Every magical warrior has corresponding cherished things and people they want to protect in their hearts. Therefore, they make a wish and bear corresponding responsibilities for that wish. Although they save many people in their fight against witches, each battle with a witch is a narrow escape from death. Thus, in continuous combat, they forget their initial expectations and hopes, accumulating resentment and jealousy. Therefore, for every despairing witch eliminated, a magical warrior will turn into a despairing witch, making the war endless.

zh.condition: 有一个契约能实现奇迹满足任何"希望",代价是成为魔法战士,究其一生背负与绝望魔女战斗的命运,守护世界,但这场战争却永无止境。

The battle against despairing witches is extremely arduous, often a narrow escape from death
In prolonged combat, magical warriors gradually forget their initial 'hope'
Magical warriors accumulate negative emotions like resentment and jealousy during battle
Magical warriors will eventually transform into new 'despairing witches' due to negative emotions, making the war endless

title: One Last Time
condition: 'This is the last time,' the man said and stuck out his tongue. Shortly after, he died, with his tongue still out.
solution: The man was in a casino. Every time he lost a bet, he would cut off a part of his body. 'The last time' referred to the fact that only his tongue was left to reach for the cards.

title: Three Phone Calls
condition: You received a third call from the teacher, telling you not to go to school anymore. You burst into tears while booking a train ticket.
solution: You are a migrant worker who left your child in your hometown. Your child, due to excellent grades, was bullied at school. Afraid of worrying her parents, she decided to hide it. To fit in, she started not doing homework, skipping classes, and deliberately answering questions incorrectly. The volunteer teacher discovered this situation and called you, but you couldn't return home. Even though the child did this, the situation didn't improve; she was still bullied by classmates, and it got worse. The child started self-harming, hoping to get your attention. The teacher called you again, and you still didn't go home. Finally, one day, the child jumped from the school building, ending her own life. You finally have to go home, to see your child one last time.

zh.title: 三个电话

zh.solution: 你是一名离乡打工人员,把孩子留在了家乡。你的孩子因为优秀的成绩受到了校园霸凌,因为害怕父母担心,她决定隐瞒这件事。为了融入集体,她开始不写作业,逃课,故意答错题,支教老师发现了这个情况,打电话给你,而你没法回老家。即便孩子这么做了,情况并没有改善,还是被同学欺负,愈演愈烈,孩子开始自残,希望得到你的关心,老师又打电话给你,你依然没有回家。终于,这一天孩子从教学楼上一跃而下结束了自己的生命,你终于要回家了,见孩子最后一面。

The child was experiencing something very painful at school (e.g., bullying, exclusion).

title: Best Friend
condition: After graduation, I hadn't seen my best friend from school. That day, she came to see me, but with a 'thud'... we were separated by life and death.
solution: She was once my best friend at a school for the blind. After graduation, we went our separate ways. I was fortunate enough to undergo surgery to restore my sight, but she was not so lucky. She said she was coming to see me after a long time. I was full of anticipation, but when I opened the door, the most familiar voice came from an ugly-looking man. I was instantly disgusted. How could I tolerate such deception? So I led him to the rooftop and pushed him off. The 'thud' was the sound of him hitting the ground.

title: Untitled #1128
condition: Two brothers are involved in a murder. Though it's clear that one of them actually committed the crime, neither can be punished. Why?
solution: One of the brothers (A) confesses to the murder. At his trial, his brother (B) is called as the only defense witness; B immediately confesses, in graphic detail, to having committed the crime. The defense lawyer refuses to have the trial stopped, and A is acquitted under the "reasonable doubt" clause. Immediately afterward, B goes on trial for the murder; A is called as the only defense witness and he confesses. B is declared innocent; and though everyone knows that one of them did it, how can they tell who? Further, neither can be convicted of perjury until it's decided which of them did it... I don't know if that would actually work under the US legal system, but someone else who heard the story said that his father was on the jury for a very similar case in New York some years ago. Mark Brader points out that the brothers might be convicted of conspiracy to commit perjury or to obstruct justice, or something of that kind.
key_facts: Both brothers confess to the crime
Each brother is the only defense witness in the other's trial
Acquitted under "reasonable doubt" clause
Possible charges of conspiracy to commit perjury or obstruct justice

title: Ответный ход
condition: Помещики часто задерживали оплату людям, строившим и ремонтировавшим дома, а то и вовсе выгоняли их без денег. Тогда строители придумали оригинальный ход, и помещики сразу начали выплачивать им сполна. Что они сделали?
solution: При закладке дома или при его ремонте они подкладывали в определенное место сырое яйцо, и через некоторое время оно начинало протухать. Если помещик вызывал мастеров, чтобы оплатить их работу, то они забирали яйцо. Если же он прогонял их, яйцо со временем начинало ужасно пахнуть, и было очень трудно найти, где оно спрятано.

en.title: The countermove
en.condition: Landlords used to delay payment to people who built and repaired houses or even booted them out without money. But the builders came up with an uncommon move, which immediately made the landlords pay them in full. What did they do?
en.solution: When laying a foundation for a house or repairing it, they put a raw egg in a certain place. After a while, it began to rot. If the landlord called the builders and paid for their work, they took the egg away. If he booted them out, the egg eventually began to stink, and it was very difficult to find where it had been hidden.

title: Half a point
condition: In the 1940s, a real football game in Brazil ended with an unusual score of 0.5 - 0. What happened?
solution:
When the score was 0-0, a penalty kick was awarded. The attacker runs up and strikes... and one of the seams of the ball, which at that time was hand-made, burst: the inner rubber bubble flew into the gate, and the outer leather case rolled out of the gate. The referee’s decision was: “half a goal.”

Since no more goals were scored, the game ended with a score of 0.5 - 0.

title: Domo Arigato?
condition:
On a clear December morning in Kansas, students filed into Science class ready for a good nap. Before the first student could get to sleep, the teacher, Mr. Roboto, walked in. Noticing his class seemed particularly sleepy this cold day, he decided that a nice trip outside should wake 'em up.

"Class! We are going to go on a little walk outside."

Hearing most of the class whine about the cold, he decided that he shouldn't be completely heartless.

"Ok, ok. We will still be going outside, but I guess we can have a little fire to keep us nice and warm."

This seemed to make the class a little more willing, so without further discussion, they all set out to the school's football field.

Mr. Roboto led them to midfield, where he seemed to have a little display set up. He turned to the class, and said:

"Class, I know you are all freezing, and are wondering when I'm going to start a fire. However, I seem to be in a bit of a jam. All I have is some water, wood shavings, and all ol' Mother Earth has to offer. How in the world could I make a fire?"

At this, most of the students began whining. But a few of them started yelling out ideas:

"Wait for it to start storming, and start one from the lightning!"; "Try to make super-hot steam!"; "Build a fire ray out of dirt and grass!" the students shouted out.

"All great guesses, but no. There is a much easier, faster way than all of that," Mr. Roboto replied.

After a few more minutes of wrong guesses, Mr. Roboto decided to go ahead and show them how to build the fire. It's your job to explain how it could be possible.
solution: Mr. Roboto used a convex lens made out of ice - a form of water. This convex lens works just like a magnifying glass, it focuses the sun's rays on the wood shavings, and in a matter of minutes starts a small fire going. He just adds a few sticks lying around, and voila: he had himself a nice warm fire. The temperature plays no factor in this, he only needed sunlight, which he got on the clear day.

title: Train Robbery
condition:
Tom was helping his father out at the printer's shop when his uncle Max ran in to tell them about the train robbery.

"It was Butch Cassidy and the Wild Bunch! They murdered Mr. Simpson! "

Tom's father was puzzled. Butch Cassidy was the Robin Hood of the area. He had never killed anyone. He and Tom went to the sheriff's office to collect some evidence for the newspaper.

"There were two witnesses. Ms. Greene was sitting next to him on the train. Right before Mr. Simpson was shot, she heard him say 'Butch'. The second witness was Lester Strong. It appears that Mr. Simpson pulled down Butch's handkerchief. Lester was able to identify him because of his days working at the saloon."

Tom decided to put his great brain to work. That night, he spent his time thinking of names that sound like Butch. His theory was that Ms. Greene had heard a name that sounded like Butch, and with Lester Strong yelling 'Butch Cassidy', she had decided that that was it. All he could come up with was Hutch.

The next morning, the sheriff told them that a total of about six thousand dollars was stolen from Mr. Simpson's money belt.

"If only we could trace those bills!" Tom muttered.

"Well, we do know that they were Kansas banknotes. But does anyone have an idea of who could have stolen the money and killed Mr. Simpson?" Tom's father said.

Tom remembered his theory. "Yes! Is there anyone by the name of Hutch nearby?" Tom told them his theory. Rewarding it was too, for there was one gambler who was now at the top of their suspect list. Hutch Pfifer.

The sheriff traced Hutch for the next few days. In fact, Hutch (the usually broke gambler) had bought his girl, Rose, an expensive dress, and lost two hundred dollars at the saloon, both in Kansas banknotes!

Tom had also realized that if it wasn't Butch Cassidy and the Wild Bunch, then there were other accomplices as well. The sheriff asked the bank to monitor Kansas banknotes. The very next day, Lester Strong turned in seven hundred dollars in Kansas banknotes.

Now the only dilemma was, how would Tom, his father, and the Sheriff prove that it wasn't Butch Cassidy?

The next day, the sheriff got some news from a nearby town. Several people, including the mayor, had met Butch on the same day as the robbery. The problem now was how to prove it was Hutch and Lester.

Tom had it all figured out. He asked the sheriff to get Mr. Simpson's mail from the hotel. Hutch and Lester were successfully put in jail. How did Tom do it?
solution: Ranchers usually paid and got paid in cash. They generally made a list of the numbers of the bills. Mr. Simpson mailed the list to himself, in case he lost it. That was all the evidence needed.
